description.para: Register offsets for the XSpi device.
This register holds the interrupt status flags for the Spi device.

This register is used to enable interrupt sources for the Spi device. Writing a '1' to a bit in this register enables the corresponding Interrupt. Writing a '0' to a bit in this register disables the corresponding Interrupt.
ISR/IER registers have the same bit definitions and are only defined once.

briefdescription.para: The following bits are available only in axi_qspi Interrupt Status and Interrupt Enable registers.

memberdef.name: XSP_INTR_ALL
memberdef.initializer: (XSP_INTR_MODE_FAULT_MASK | \ XSP_INTR_SLAVE_MODE_FAULT_MASK | \ XSP_INTR_TX_EMPTY_MASK | \ XSP_INTR_TX_UNDERRUN_MASK | \ XSP_INTR_RX_FULL_MASK | \ XSP_INTR_TX_HALF_EMPTY_MASK | \ XSP_INTR_RX_OVERRUN_MASK | \ XSP_INTR_SLAVE_MODE_MASK | \ XSP_INTR_RX_NOT_EMPTY_MASK | \ XSP_INTR_CMD_ERR_MASK | \ XSP_INTR_LOOP_BACK_ERR_MASK | \ XSP_INTR_MSB_ERR_MASK | \ XSP_INTR_SLAVE_MODE_ERR_MASK | \ XSP_INTR_CPOL_CPHA_ERR_MASK)

briefdescription.para: Mask for all the interrupts in the IP Interrupt Registers.
The interrupts we want at startup.

detaileddescription.para: We add the TX_EMPTY interrupt in later when we're getting ready to transfer data. The others we don't care about for now.

The default data format is MSB first. The LSB first data format is not available in all versions of the Xilinx Spi Device whereas the MSB first data format is supported by all the versions of the Xilinx Spi Devices. Please check the HW specification to see if this feature is supported or not.

sectiondef.header: SPI Control Register (CR) masks for XIP Mode
Status Register (SR) masks for XIP Mode

briefdescription.para: Tx/Rx Reg is Half Word (16 bit) Wide.
Tx/Rx Reg is Word (32 bit) Wide.

description.para: The following constants define the modes in which qxi_qspi operates.

briefdescription.para: Read from the specified Spi device register.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device.
contains the offset from the 1st register of the device to select the specific register.

simplesect.para: The value read from the register.
C-Style signature: u32 XSpi_ReadReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset);

briefdescription.para: Write to the specified Spi device register.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device.
contains the offset from the 1st register of the device to select the specific register.
is the value to be written to the register.

simplesect.para: C-Style signature: void XSpi_WriteReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset, u32 RegisterValue);
